body.title: CLOSED: queue.json drop — stage B: migrate readers + remove echo + delete file
body.result: all queue.json readers migrated to load_tasks(); _write_queue_echo/_check_drift removed; runtime queue.json deleted; 4148 tests pass
body.description:
Stage B follow-on to T-cc-queue-write-race (stage A shipped 2026-05-03). Stage A eliminated the drift sources (cert_worker_freeze, worker_foreman, ops.queue_task all routed through cc_queue.save_tasks); this stage completes the deletion: migrate every reader from queue.json to cc_queue.load_tasks(), remove _write_queue_echo from cc_queue._save, delete queue.json from disk.

**Use callmap.md as planning input** — it lists every queue.json toucher.

**Readers to migrate (use cc_queue.load_tasks):**
- lab/claudecode/github_sync.py
- lab/claudecode/slate_manager.py
- lab/claudecode/audit_pass1_run.py (auditing the file shape — may legitimately need queue.json bytes; review)
- lab/claudecode/stale_ticket_sweeper.py
- lab/claudecode/audit_ticket_shape.py (auditing file shape — same review)
- lab/claudecode/map_igor.py
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/action_claim_verifier.py (uses MTIME for timing signal — this MAY be legitimate; review carefully)
- wild_igor/igor/dashboard/terminal.py
- wild_igor/igor/tools/goal_continuation.py
- wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (HIGH-inertia — pre-approval required)

**Tests to update:**
- tests/test_queue_consumer.py
- tests/test_pe_entry_nodes.py (uses _QUEUE_FILE patch)

**Then:**
- Remove _write_queue_echo and the call to it in _save()
- Remove `import json` if no longer needed for echo
- Delete ~/.TheIgors/cc_channel/queue.json
- Update CLAUDE.md if queue.json is mentioned anywhere

**Open question for review (audit_pass1_run + audit_ticket_shape):** these scripts audit the file SHAPE of queue.json. If queue.json no longer exists, these checks become "audit Postgres state" instead — different shape. May need to keep queue.json as periodic export OR just rewrite the audit to query Postgres.

**Open question (action_claim_verifier mtime):** uses queue.json mtime as a timing signal for "recent ticket activity." Without queue.json, need an equivalent signal — maybe Postgres metadata.updated_at MAX over clan.memories. Or keep queue.json as a side-effect timestamp file.

**Affected files:** see callmap.md sections for cc_queue.* and the readers listed above; tests as listed; CLAUDE.md if mentioned.

**Design rules:** docs-in-code; no-silent-data-loss (drift detection in stage A stays — it should warn even if no readers exist); test-plan-required.

**Scope boundary:** IN — the readers listed; remove echo write; delete file. OUT — Postgres schema changes; cc_queue.py CLI rewrite (still works as wrapper around load_tasks/save_tasks).

**Test plan:** all queue-touching tests still pass after migration. New test that drift detection in _save still fires WARNING when given a synthetic load+modify+save sequence (proves stage A safety net works post-Stage-B too).

**Why this is a separate ticket:** Stage A is the load-bearing safety win (drift class eliminated). Stage B is the cleanup. Splitting lets stage A ship clean and reviewed; stage B inherits Akien attention separately.
